message:
feat(server): connection manager refinements + beads sync

Expand ConnectionManager with additional connection tracking and
lifecycle management improvements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-221m","title":"Service Layer Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, middleware)","description":"# Service Layer Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the service layer (lnm, tower equivalent) ensuring composability, cancel-correctness, and middleware stack behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Service trait: call, poll_ready
- Layer trait: composition
- ServiceBuilder: layer chaining
- Standard middleware: timeout, rate limit, retry, concurrency limit

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Basic service call | Request/response lifecycle |
| Timeout middleware | Budget integration, timeout enforcement |
| Rate limiting | Request throttling, backpressure |
| Retry with backoff | Exponential backoff, max attempts |
| Circuit breaker | Open/half-open/closed states |
| Bulkhead isolation | Resource partitioning |
| Middleware stack | Correct layering order |

### 3. Cancel-Safety Tests
- Cancel mid-request: clean abort
- Cancel with pending retries: no orphan requests
- Cancel with rate limit queue: queue cleanup

### 4. Load Tests
- Concurrent requests
- Backpressure behavior
- Resource exhaustion handling

## Logging Requirements
- Middleware events logged with request IDs
- On failure: dump middleware stack state
- Request/response timings logged

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All middleware have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass with real HTTP backend
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for all middleware
- [ ] Load tests pass without resource leaks
- [ ] `cargo test service` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib service
cargo test --test service_e2e
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:47:11.707559049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:17:19.182769279Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:17:19.182686816Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-221m","depends_on_id":"asupersync-lnm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22vb","title":"Codec Framework Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, framing)","description":"# Codec Framework Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the codec framework (4nz, tokio-util codecs equivalent) ensuring correct encoding/decoding and framing.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Decoder trait: decode, decode_eof
- Encoder trait: encode
- FramedRead: framed decoding
- FramedWrite: framed encoding
- Framed: bidirectional codec
- Common codecs: LinesCodec, BytesCodec, LengthDelimitedCodec

### 2. Codec Implementation Tests
- Lines: newline delimiter
- Bytes: raw passthrough
- LengthDelimited: length-prefixed frames
- JSON: serde_json codec
- Custom codec patterns

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Line-delimited protocol | Text protocol parsing |
| Length-prefixed frames | Binary protocol framing |
| Partial frame handling | Buffer management |
| Large frame handling | Memory efficiency |
| Cancel mid-frame | Clean abort |
| Codec chaining | Nested framing |
| Error propagation | Decode error handling |

### 4. Edge Cases
- Empty frames
- Maximum frame size
- Incomplete frames at EOF
- Malformed input
- Concurrent encode/decode

### 5. Performance Tests
- Throughput for various codecs
- Memory allocation per frame
- Zero-copy where possible

## Logging Requirements
- Codec events logged with frame sizes
- On failure: dump buffer state, codec state
- Frame metrics: encoded, decoded, errors

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All codecs have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass
- [ ] Edge cases handled correctly
- [ ] Performance baselines established
- [ ] `cargo test codec` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib codec
cargo test --test codec_e2e
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"HazyRidge","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:49:24.986532463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:07:56.254159284Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22vb","depends_on_id":"asupersync-4nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2520","title":"Pool integration tests incorrectly use try_acquire expecting resource creation","description":"Tests in tests/pool_tests.rs call try_acquire() expecting it to create new resources, but try_acquire() only returns idle resources.  {"id":"bd-39ik","title":"HTTP Server/Client Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, protocol)","description":"# HTTP Server/Client Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the HTTP layer (if7, hyper equivalent) ensuring protocol compliance, cancel-correctness, and performance.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Request parsing: methods, headers, body
- Response construction: status, headers, body
- HTTP/1.1: keep-alive, chunked encoding, pipelining
- HTTP/2: streams, flow control, HPACK
- Connection management: pooling, reuse

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| GET request/response | Basic HTTP lifecycle |
| POST with body | Request body handling |
| Streaming response | Chunked transfer |
| Keep-alive reuse | Connection pooling |
| HTTP/2 multiplexing | Stream concurrency |
| Cancel mid-request | Client abort handling |
| Server shutdown | Graceful drain |
| Large body transfer | Memory efficiency |

### 3. Protocol Compliance Tests
- RFC 9110 (HTTP Semantics)
- RFC 9112 (HTTP/1.1)
- RFC 9113 (HTTP/2)
- Header validation
- Content-Length vs Transfer-Encoding

### 4. Performance Tests
- Requests per second baseline
- Concurrent connection handling
- Memory usage under load
- Latency distribution (p50, p95, p99)

### 5. Security Tests
- Request smuggling prevention
- Header injection prevention
- Timeout enforcement
- Resource exhaustion protection

## Logging Requirements
- HTTP events logged with request IDs
- On failure: dump connection state, pending requests
- Timing breakdown: parsing, processing, serialization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Protocol compliance tests pass
- [ ] E2E scenarios cover HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for client and server
- [ ] Performance baselines established
- [ ] `cargo test http` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib http
cargo test --test http_e2e
cargo bench --bench http_perf
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:47:38.086653259Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T05:40:35.738379666Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T05:40:35.738315567Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39ik","depends_on_id":"asupersync-if7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
